triageFlags.title: Excessive Loan-to-Value (LTV) Ratio
triageFlags.description: The LTV ratio exceeds the maximum allowable limit for the applicant's credit tier.
triageFlags.recommendation: Decline the proposal as the LTV ratio is significantly above the policy limit.
triageFlags.reasoning: According to the underwriting policy, the maximum LTV for Tier A applicants is 110%. The current LTV of 224.44% is more than double the permissible limit, indicating a high risk of over-leverage.

triageFlags.title: Excessive Debt-to-Income (DTI) Ratio
triageFlags.description: The DTI ratio exceeds the maximum allowable limit for the applicant's credit tier.
triageFlags.recommendation: Decline the proposal as the DTI ratio is above the policy threshold.
triageFlags.reasoning: The underwriting policy specifies a maximum DTI of 45% for Tier A applicants. The current DTI of 55% indicates that the applicant's debt obligations are too high relative to their income, posing a significant risk of default.

content.reasoning: The analysis identified that the LTV ratio is 224.44%, far exceeding the maximum allowable 110% for Tier A applicants. Additionally, the DTI ratio is 55%, which is above the 45% limit for Tier A. These critical violations indicate a high risk of financial instability and non-compliance with underwriting policies.
